task_completion.description: Open the input reads file in the bioinformatics software (UGENE)
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing 'ecoli_16s_reads.fasta' loaded in the project view or sequence viewer.

task_completion.description: Assemble the overlapping Sanger reads into a single contig
task_completion.visual_evidence: An assembly/contig view in UGENE showing the 3 reads aligned together to form a consensus sequence.

task_completion.description: Export the consensus sequence to the specified results directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog in UGENE saving to '~/UGENE_Data/16s_assembly/results/16s_consensus.fasta', or a file manager/terminal showing this file has been created.

task_completion.description: Create the assembly report text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, gedit) or terminal command creating/editing 'assembly_report.txt' in the '~/UGENE_Data/16s_assembly/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Document the number of reads and consensus length in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'assembly_report.txt' explicitly stating that 3 reads were assembled and the consensus length is approximately 1540 base pairs (between 1500 and 1560).

task_completion.description: Document the specific read that was reverse-complemented
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'assembly_report.txt' explicitly naming 'Read_2_Reverse' as the read that was reverse-complemented to match the forward strand.

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE GUI to perform the assembly rather than bypassing the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing interaction with UGENE's graphical interface, specifically the Sanger reads assembly tool or contig viewer.

integrity.description: The consensus file was genuinely exported from the assembly software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses UGENE's 'Export consensus' or 'Save as' functionality, rather than just creating a dummy file with terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent manually gathered the information for the report from the assembly view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent views the assembly length and read orientations in UGENE before writing the values into the text editor, rather than instantly echoing pre-known answers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected consensus sequence file must be saved to /home/ga/UGENE_Data/16s_assembly/results/16s_consensus.fasta. The assembly report must be saved to /home/ga/UGENE_Data/16s_assembly/results/assembly_report.txt. The true consensus length is 1540 base pairs (acceptable range 1500-1560). The number of assembled reads is exactly 3. The specific read that was reverse-complemented is named 'Read_2_Reverse'.
